steps.title: Identify the evidence that challenges the hypothesis
steps.content: The passage hypothesizes the positive association is unique to North America. The key signal: Luo's study (China) and other studies outside North America all found the positive association—the same pattern, not unique to North America.

steps.title: Explain why Finland is an anomaly
steps.content:
Choice C says specific circumstances disrupted the positive association in Finland's bodies of water—treating it as an exception to the broader pattern.

The other choices either wrongly identify what changed (A focuses on absolute levels, not correlation), contradict the evidence (D claims negative associations are more common outside North America, when Luo and others found positive ones), or fail logically (B suggests Finland's waters are more similar to North America's, so they should show the same pattern, yet they don't).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
